description:
You are a medical scientist studying cross-species conservation of the p53 tumor suppressor protein to identify universally conserved functional domains. The protein sequences of 8 vertebrate p53 orthologs have been downloaded for you.

Perform the following multi-step analysis in UGENE:

1. Open the file ~/UGENE_Data/p53/p53_orthologs.fasta in UGENE.

2. Perform a multiple sequence alignment using the MUSCLE algorithm (do NOT use ClustalW or MAFFT; use the MUSCLE default settings).

3. Export the resulting alignment in Stockholm format and save it to ~/UGENE_Data/p53/results/p53_alignment.sto

4. Export the same alignment in FASTA (aligned) format and save it to ~/UGENE_Data/p53/results/p53_alignment.fasta (make sure the sequences contain the gap characters).

5. Extract/generate the consensus sequence from the alignment. Export it to ~/UGENE_Data/p53/results/p53_consensus.fasta in FASTA format. Make sure the header line of this file contains the word 'consensus'.

6. Create a plain text conservation report at ~/UGENE_Data/p53/results/p53_conservation_report.txt. This report MUST include:
   - The total number of sequences aligned (should be 8).
   - The alignment length (total number of columns/positions).
   - The number of fully conserved positions (positions where all 8 sequences share the identical residue).
   - A brief qualitative description of which regions appear most conserved (e.g., DNA-binding domain, N-terminal, or C-terminal).
   - The consensus sequence string itself pasted within the report.
